title: Red Bull confirm major change as Lando Norris handed Spa setback
summary:
Red Bull confirmed it will return to an old rear wing for this weekend's Belgian Grand Prix, dropping its upside-down rear wing following Max Verstappen's recent accidents. It was revealed by Laurent Mekies, that in post-Silverstone tests an issue had been discovered.

Also on media day at Spa-Francorchamps, McLaren confirmed that Lando Norris would take a 10-place grid penalty into Sunday's race for taking a fourth power electronics, but is at least now on Mercedes' latest version.

Join RacingNews365's Nick Golding and Samuel Coop as they dissect media day at the Belgian GP!
